purpose: Compact decision map for a potential custom tools / workflow-products service line. Use before any implementation pass; do not replace batch-1/batch-2 maps.

decision.summary: Darling MarTech should treat custom tools and small workflow products as a sellable specialty aligned with systems work — framed around demand capture and operational clarity, not generic app development or portfolio sprawl.

slugAlternativesAssessed.notes: Buyer-readable, maps to offer title, distinct from generic custom-apps or internal-tools-only phrasing; matches kebab length of agentic-marketing-systems.
Shorter but slightly narrower — can sound like martech stack plugins rather than workflow products and surfaces.
Omits 'tools' — still valid if title on page stays full; slightly less obvious in cluster link labels.

flatSlugRedirect.notes: Add permanent redirect in next.config.js mirroring agentic-marketing-systems pattern so bookmarks and flat links resolve.

implementationPlan.runtimePattern: Nested page under app/services/systems/<slug>/page.tsx loads entry from allServicePages by id (same as agentic-marketing-systems). standaloneServicePages entry uses routePath !== /services/{id} so generateServiceStaticParams excludes it.

timing.shippedNote: Runtime page, data entry, redirect, and helpers landed 2026-04-02. `/services` cluster link still intentionally omitted until chartered.
timing.proofRationale: graston-growth-engine + barbershop-command-center are sufficient for an initial proof grid; no need to delay on proof prep unless copy strategy changes.

servicesIndexStrategy.rationale: Fourth systems cluster link added (`Custom tools & workflow products` → nested canonical URL) for discoverability without redesigning the index layout.

futureFilesToTouchMinimal: data/services.ts — new standaloneServicePages object (id custom-tools-workflow-products, routePath, layer build, proofWorkSlugs, relatedServiceSlugs, signs/deliverables/faq when copy ready); serviceDetails systems parent childServiceSlugs
app/services/systems/custom-tools-workflow-products/page.tsx — new file, mirror agentic-marketing-systems page (metadata + ServiceDetailPage)
next.config.js — redirects() entry flat /services/custom-tools-workflow-products → nested canonical
docs/context/project/service-pages/service-proof-snippets.json — add services.<slug> primary/supporting lines when copy locked
app/sitemap.ts — no manual change if allServicePages continues to drive URLs (new entry auto-included)
CLAUDE.md — swap planning/deferred note for shipped status when live
Optional: data/services.ts contactServiceOptions if contact form should list the new offer by name

preferredLabel.title: Custom Tools & Workflow Products
preferredLabel.rationale: Keeps the offer adjacent to CRM/automation ('workflow') and recognizable buyer language ('tools', 'products') without reading as a dev shop or 'we build apps.'

namingOptionsReviewed.label: Custom Tools & Workflow Products
namingOptionsReviewed.notes: Broad enough for internal dashboards, customer-facing configurators, and interactive surfaces; still outcome-anchored.

namingOptionsReviewed.label: Custom Marketing & Operations Tools
namingOptionsReviewed.notes: Clear demand-gen tie; slightly more verbose; good for body copy or parent deliverable bullets.

namingOptionsReviewed.label: Bespoke Web Applications
namingOptionsReviewed.notes: Reads generic dev-shop; invites RFP-style scope creep.

namingOptionsReviewed.label: Interactive Experiences / Products
namingOptionsReviewed.notes: Portfolio- and agency-flavored; weak commercial specificity for SMB buyers.

belongsInSystemIf: The build closes a measurable gap in demand capture, qualification, handoff, or day-to-day execution (not 'nice to have' software).
It connects to the existing stack story: CRM, site, measurement, or automation — accountable owner stays Darling MarTech-shaped.
Proof can be anchored on production client work, not internal experiments or /tools utilities.
doesNotBelongIf: The primary motivation is showing more builds on /work or padding portfolio depth.
The buyer only wants commodity engineering with no marketing/systems accountability.
The same story is already the hero of website-strategy or agentic-marketing-systems without adding a distinct purchase.

clusterFit.rationale: Parent systems copy and deliverables already include custom calculators, dashboards, and internal tools; this specialty makes that line item conversion-ready without inventing a new top-level cluster.

recommendedSystemFit.description: Mirror Batch 2 nested pattern: future slug as standaloneServicePages entry with routePath under /services/systems/… (like agentic-marketing-systems). Not a new parent in serviceDetails. Do not add a fifth /services cluster.
recommendedSystemFit.alternateIfNarrower: If a full page is deferred long-term, reinforce positioning only inside crm-architecture + agentic-marketing-systems copy — secondary to a dedicated page.

candidateProofDirection.notes: Graston Growth Engine is the strongest primary: production two-sided surface, integrations, AI-assisted workflow. Barbershop Command Center supports dual-sided operational UI without duplicating the entire CRM-architecture story.

nextImplementationSlice: Copy pass (layered writing rule) then data/services.ts standalone + systems.childServiceSlugs.
parent-child-linking-kit.json + service-proof-snippets.json for the new slug.
ServicesExperience.tsx systems cluster fourth link — only after page exists and is explicitly chartered.
